messages.content:
30. Which ONE of the following is the safest way to keep chilled food before serving?
 in a warm place where they can be easily collected for serving below 41°F near an open window where it will be coo

Below 41°F

Chilled food should be kept at or below 41°F (5°C) to ensure safety before serving. This helps to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause foodborne illnesses. Storing food in a warm place or near an open window does not provide the necessary cold temperature to keep the food safe and could expose the food to the danger zone temperatures (between 40°F - 140°F or 4°C - 60°C), where bacteria multiply rapidly.
It looks like your message may have been cut off. If you have any further questions or topics you would like to discuss or clarify, feel free to ask!

30. Which ONE of the following is the safest way to keep chilled food before serving?
 in a warm place where they can be easily collected for serving below 41°F near an open window where it will be coo

The safest way to keep chilled food before serving is "below 41°F." 

Keeping food at or below this temperature helps prevent the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause foodborne illness. Chilled food should not be kept in warm places or near open windows where it could enter the "danger zone" (temperatures between 40°F and 140°F), where bacteria can grow rapidly.

32. By law your employer must provide:
 color coded cutting boards and utensils a separate hand wash sink dedicated knives time off with pay for staff who get sick

By law, your employer must provide:

A separate hand wash sink.

It is a legal requirement in many jurisdictions for food service establishments to have a designated hand washing sink to ensure employees can maintain proper hygiene. This is to prevent contamination of food and to promote sanitary conditions in the food preparation area.

Additionally, depending on the local health codes and regulations, there might be requirements for color-coded cutting boards and utensils to prevent cross-contamination, as well as guidelines for handling situations when staff get sick (which may or may not include time off with pay). However, the provision of a separate hand wash sink is a commonly mandated sanitary requirement across many regions.

32. By law your employer must provide:
 color coded cutting boards and utensils a separate hand wash sink dedicated knives time off with pay for staff who get sick

By law, your employer must provide:

A separate hand wash sink.

This is mandated by health codes in many locations to ensure employees can maintain proper hygiene, specifically to prevent the contamination of food with pathogens that could be present on their hands. The availability of a hand washing sink dedicated exclusively for employees helps to enforce proper handwashing practices within food service establishments.

Other items listed, such as color-coded cutting boards and utensils, dedicated knives, and time off with pay for staff who get sick, may be best practices or even required in some jurisdictions to prevent cross-contamination and handle employee illness. However, these requirements can vary widely by region and are not as universally mandated as the requirement for a hand wash sink. Always check with your local health department and labor laws to understand the specific legal obligations for employers in your area.

 65°F 85°F 90°F 135°F

36. Which ONE of the following statements about pathogenic bacteria is CORRECT?
 pathogenic bacteria grows best at 98°F bacteria can be seen without a microscope all bacteria are harmful food poisoning bacteria can not grow below 20°F

38. Which of the following would cause PHYSICAL contamination?
 glass from a broken light bulb pathogenic bacteria on chicken detergent left on a food surface a mushroom
hurry up and answer please
Glass from a broken light bulb.

 glass from a broken light bulb pathogenic bacteria on chicken detergent left on a food surface a mushroom

39. Which ONE of the following is the primary reason for the practice of CLEAN AS YOU GO?
 it saves time at the end of the day it reduces cross-contamination it allocates cleaning responsibilities across the team it reminds everyone of the importance of a clean kitchen
no this one
